reasoning: The informal statement is a chain of iffs "A ↔ B ↔ C" where A: xy=yx, B: y⁻¹xy=x, C: x⁻¹y⁻¹xy=1. The standard reading of a chain of iffs is "A ↔ B and B ↔ C", which is exactly what the Lean statement encodes as the conjunction `(A ↔ B) ∧ (B ↔ C)`. This is logically equivalent to all three being pairwise equivalent (transitivity of iff gives A ↔ C automatically). The group operations, inverses, and identity in the Lean expressions correspond exactly to the informal equations, and the statement is universally quantified over arbitrary groups G and arbitrary elements x, y, matching the informal text.
